verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with a KDEL retention signal, so its baseline localization is the ER lumen. However, it is well-documented that under cellular stress conditions — particularly in cancer cells, during ER stress/UPR activation, and during viral infection — GRP78 translocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Cancer-cell surface GRP78 is a validated therapeutic target: multiple antibodies (e.g., anti-GRP78 antibodies in clinical/preclinical development) and CAR-T programs specifically target cell-surface GRP78 on tumor cells. Surface biotinylation and flow cytometry studies confirm its presence on the outer leaflet of stressed and malignant cells. This is a canonical example of a stress/oncogenic-state ecto-chaperone. The protein also appears on the surface of apoptotic/ICD cells. Mechanism of translocation is not fully understood but the surface pool is wash-resistant and distinct from the soluble ER pool. Ruling out canonical surface: no TM domain, not GPI-anchored, not a classical surface receptor.
